Title: Deciphering the Cryptic Genome: Genome-wide Analyses of the Rice Pathogen Fusarium fujikuroi Reveal Complex Regulation of Secondary Metabolism and Novel Metabolites

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: culture filtrate; induced during rice root infection at transcript level for cluster
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low basal expression in vitro; cluster induced upon overexpression; associated with rice infection context via PKS19 cluster expression
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpression enhanced expression of PKS19, FFUJ_12240, FFUJ_12242 and itself, but not FFUJ_12241 or FFUJ_12244
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a pathway-specific activator required to induce production of PKS19 cluster-derived metabolites; overexpression strongly increases novel compounds, especially with PKS19 co-overexpression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene overexpression; Northern blot of cluster genes; HPLC-FTMS metabolite profiling; in planta qPCR for PKS19-linked cluster expression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of FFUJ_12243 activated transcription of multiple PKS19 cluster genes. 2. FFUJ_12243 overexpression enabled production of four novel metabolites absent from wild type. 3. Co-overexpression with PKS19 increased metabolite levels about 10-fold over TF overexpression alone.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen availability and plant infection context (rice versus maize roots)
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Cluster region showed minimal H3K9ac and some H3K4me2 at FFUJ_12243; flanking AT-rich regions enriched in H3K9me3 under low-expression conditions
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray transcriptomics: GEO GSE43745; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE46033; HPLC-FTMS metabolomics
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although a transcription factor, it is not the main nutritional metabolite regulator focus of the paper across broader pathway regulation, but is a core cluster gene experimentally manipulated for product discovery; categorized here as Common_Gene per tool schema emphasis on non-master regulators when pathway-cluster activation is the main practical function.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for activating cryptic fungal polyketide clusters and discovering silent metabolites through transcriptional engineering
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Putative role in rice infection/pathogen adaptation; exact bioactivity not yet known

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant growth hormones; contribute to bakanae disease and invasive growth in rice roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fungal culture filtrate; produced during rice infection context
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen limitation; acidic versus alkaline conditions; rice versus maize infection
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts geranylgeranyl diphosphate to ent-copalyl diphosphate and then to ent-kaurene, the first committed intermediate in fungal GA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly expressed under low nitrogen; significantly higher during rice root infection than maize
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced under acidic and alkaline low-nitrogen conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes gibberellin biosynthesis, which contributes to invasive growth of F. fujikuroi in rice root tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR in planta; comparative metabolite profiling; cluster deletion mutant SG139 lacking GA cluster; transformation of F. oxysporum with F. fujikuroi GA cluster; proteomics
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPS/KS is expressed strongly under low nitrogen and more highly on rice than maize roots. 2. Its product ent-kaurene is the first committed GA intermediate. 3. Functional GA biosynthesis is restricted to F. fujikuroi among examined species despite broader cluster presence.
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: GA cluster expression correlates with H3K9ac enrichment under inducing low-nitrogen conditions; P450-4 and P450-2 also enriched for H3K4me2
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray transcriptomics: GEO GSE43745; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE46033; proteomics; GC-MS/HPLC metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GA cluster presence in related species does not predict GA production; plant-specific signaling appears important
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for controlling bakanae disease by disrupting fungal GA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of GA reduces invasive symplastic growth in rice roots but not initial root colonization

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: culture filtrate; transcript strongly induced in rice roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High nitrogen weakly induces in vitro; rice versus maize infection strongly affects expression
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Novel C12 oxygenated polyketide metabolites (compounds 1–4; formulas C12H16O4, C12H18O5, C12H18O4)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes the polyketide backbone for four newly detected PKS19-cluster metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Very low in liquid culture; highly expressed in rice roots and nearly undetectable in maize roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Weak expression under some high-nitrogen conditions; strongly enhanced when cluster TF is overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required enzymatic core for production of four novel metabolites; cluster likely contributes specifically to rice infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene overexpression alone and combined with FFUJ_12243 overexpression; Northern blot; HPLC-FTMS; in planta qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PKS19 is unique to F. fujikuroi and resides in a six-gene cluster. 2. Co-overexpression with the cluster TF enabled production of four novel metabolites not detected in wild type. 3. PKS19 transcripts accumulate strongly on rice but not maize roots, suggesting a rice-specific role.
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Cluster region shows limited H3K9ac; FFUJ_12243 enriched for H3K4me2; flanking AT-rich regions enriched in H3K9me3
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray transcriptomics: GEO GSE43745; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE46033; HPLC-FTMS metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cluster absent from other examined Fusarium genomes and may have been acquired by horizontal transfer
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful model for activating silent fungal polyketide pathways; possible target to reduce rice-specific virulence factors
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Metabolite structures and host effects remain unresolved

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely bioactive secondary metabolite; related to histone deacetylase inhibitor apicidin
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Acidic high-nitrogen conditions; maize versus rice infection context affects transcript levels
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: amino acid building blocks including N-methoxy-tryptophan, pipecolic acid, phenylalanine and an amino-acid-like moiety
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Apicidin-like compound with molecular formula C35H42O7N5
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses amino acid substrates into an apicidin-like cyclic peptide scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed under acidic high-nitrogen conditions; APS1 transcript levels higher on maize than rice roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High-nitrogen induced; correlates with cluster protein detection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables biosynthesis of an apicidin-like metabolite rather than apicidin in F. fujikuroi
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics/synteny; overexpression of APS2 and heterologous APS8; HPLC-FTMS; amino acid hydrolysis analysis; transcriptomics; proteomics
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NRPS31 is unique to F. fujikuroi among examined Fusarium species and is homologous to apicidin synthetase APS1. 2. Overexpression of APS2 together with F. semitectum APS8 induced an apicidin-like metabolite with formula C35H42O7N5. 3. APS1 expression is higher on maize than rice roots, indicating plant-specific regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: gpd promoter; constitutive for APS2 and APS8 overexpression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: APS2; APS5; APS8 (heterologous F. semitectum APS8 used functionally); APS3; APS4; APS6; APS7; APS9; APS11; APS12
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Cluster shows increased H3K9ac under high nitrogen and H3K4me2 at APS5; no strong H3K9me3 over cluster itself
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray transcriptomics: GEO GSE43745; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE46033; proteomics; HPLC-FTMS metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: F. fujikuroi lacks APS10 homologue and has a likely defective APS8, likely explaining production of an apicidin-like derivative instead of apicidin
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates activation of cryptic NRPS clusters for natural product discovery
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Exact ecological function during infection is unresolved

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely bioactive secondary metabolite related to apicidin
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of the APS cluster sufficiently to enable production of an apicidin-like compound when combined with APS8 complementation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive pathway-specific activator for the APS/NRPS31 cluster
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of APS2 together with F. semitectum APS8 induces accumulation of an apicidin-like metabolite absent from wild type
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene overexpression; HPLC-FTMS; transcriptomics; comparative pathway reconstruction
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. APS2 is a positive-acting Zn(2)C6 transcription factor homologous to the activator in the F. semitectum apicidin cluster. 2. APS2 overexpression together with APS8 enabled production of an apicidin-like metabolite in F. fujikuroi. 3. APS2 therefore functions as a decisive activator of the otherwise cryptic NRPS31/APS cluster.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: High nitrogen conditions correlate with cluster activation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cluster active under acidic high-nitrogen conditions
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: APS cluster activation correlates with H3K9ac enrichment under high nitrogen
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray transcriptomics: GEO GSE43745; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE46033; HPLC-FTMS metabolomics
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: APS8 defect in F. fujikuroi limits native apicidin production, so APS2 activation alone was not sufficient in the reported experiment
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful transcriptional engineering tool to activate silent fungal NRPS clusters
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Activation may produce unknown bioactive metabolites with toxic properties
